title: Andromeda Treats Ads With Similar First 3 Seconds as Identical
insight: Under Meta's Andromeda system, two ads are treated as 'identical' if they share similar first 3 seconds — even if copy, message, or CTA differs. Same creator + same setting = same ad in Meta's eyes. Slight copy variations aren't recognized as a new concept. This means creative iteration must focus on visual differentiation in the opening hook, not just messaging changes.
raw_excerpt: Meta now treats two ads as 'identical' if they share the similar first 3 seconds.. even if the copy, message or CTA is different. Same creator + same setting = same ad. Slight copy variations isn't recognised as a new concept. Visual similarity overrides narrative difference.
source_title: Key insight from a creative workshop we ran with Meta
actionable_steps: Audit your ad library for ads sharing similar first 3 seconds — these are competing against each other
Ensure each ad has a visually distinct opening hook (different creator, setting, or format)
Stop relying on copy-only variations as a creative testing strategy

title: Visual Similarity Overrides Narrative Difference in Ad Ranking
insight: Meta's Andromeda model evaluates creative similarity primarily through visual signals, not narrative or copy. Two ads with different messages but the same visual style, creator, and setting are treated as duplicates. This means the creative differentiation spectrum ranges from 'similar message, same style' (penalized) to 'differentiated message and differentiated style' (rewarded). Ads sitting on the low-differentiation end will hit reach ceilings.
raw_excerpt: Visual similarity overrides narrative difference. If your ads are sitting left of centre on this chart, you're probably already feeling the ceiling. Meta doesn't just want more ads. It wants more variation and it's getting ruthless about what counts.
source_title: Key insight from a creative workshop we ran with Meta
actionable_steps: Map your active ads on the differentiation spectrum: same message/same style vs differentiated message/differentiated style
Prioritize differentiated style (new scenes, locations, creators) over just differentiated messaging
Track reach and frequency metrics to detect when similarity is capping delivery

title: Persona-Based Ads Unlock New Audiences 89% of the Time
insight: Meta now rewards creative built around personas, not platforms. Different motivators unlock new audiences 89% of the time. Persona-based ads perform better and last longer because they're not re-skins — they're built to resonate with fundamentally different types of people. This shifts the creative strategy from 'make more ads' to 'make ads for more types of people'.
raw_excerpt: The big shift: Meta now rewards creative built around personas, not platforms. Different motivators unlock new audiences 89% of the time. It confirms what we've seen inside accounts over the past 6 months: Persona-based ads perform better and last longer.
source_title: Key insight from a creative workshop we ran with Meta
actionable_steps: Define 3-5 distinct audience personas with different motivators for your product
Build separate creative concepts targeting each persona's unique motivations
Measure reach overlap between persona-based ad sets to confirm you're accessing new audiences

title: Push Bigger Visual Swings After a Winner to Escape Similarity Penalties
insight: Once you have a winning ad format, the instinct is to iterate incrementally. But under Andromeda's similarity detection, incremental iterations (same creator, same setting, different copy) are treated as the same ad. Instead, rethink scenes, locations, and setups entirely to escape similarity penalties while preserving the winning concept.
raw_excerpt: Push bigger visual swings after a winner emerges – Once we've got a winning format, the instinct is to go incremental – But now we're rethinking scenes, locations, setups entirely to escape similarity penalties.
source_title: Key insight from a creative workshop we ran with Meta
actionable_steps: When iterating on a winner, change the visual environment (location, lighting, setting) not just the script
Use different creators or actors for each iteration to avoid visual fingerprint overlap
Keep the winning concept/angle but rebuild the visual execution from scratch

title: Change the Hook to Change the Audience
insight: Since Meta treats the first 3 seconds as the ad's identity under Andromeda, changing the hook effectively creates a new ad in Meta's system. This lets you tap into new motivators and reach new audience segments without remaking the entire asset. The hook is the cheapest lever for creative differentiation.
raw_excerpt: Change the hook to change the audience – First 3 seconds = new ad in Meta's eyes – Use this to tap into new motivators without remaking the whole asset.
source_title: Key insight from a creative workshop we ran with Meta
actionable_steps: Create 3-5 different hooks for each winning ad body to multiply reach
Target different motivators/pain points in each hook variant
Test hooks that are visually distinct (different scene, different creator opening) not just text overlays

title: Layer Persona Insights Into Creative Briefs
insight: Creative briefs should be built around demographic and psychographic differences, not just product benefits. Brief hooks and visual styles based on persona motivators to ensure each creative is genuinely differentiated at the concept level. This prevents the common trap of producing volume that looks different on paper but identical to Andromeda.
raw_excerpt: Layer persona insights directly into creative briefs – We're now briefing hooks and styles based on demographic and psychographic differences, not just product benefits.
source_title: Key insight from a creative workshop we ran with Meta
actionable_steps: Add persona profiles (demographics, psychographics, motivators) to every creative brief
Specify distinct visual styles and hooks per persona in the brief
Review briefs before production to ensure no two personas share the same visual treatment
